title: carlina vulgaris subsp. longifolia
intro: Carlina vulgaris subsp. longifolia, also known as the Carline Thistle, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family that is native to Europe and parts of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and prevent infection. The health benefits of Carlina vulgaris subsp. longifolia include its ability to support digestive health, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Carlina vulgaris subsp. longifolia include infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which can be made from the leaves, flowers, and roots of the plant.
